data.title: Union Pacific Merger Setback Highlights Rising Rail Regulatory And Investor Risks
data.description:
• Federal regulators rejected the initial Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern merger application.
  • The Surface Transportation Board requested a revised filing with additional information.
  • Analysts reacted with downgrades, citing higher uncertainty around the proposed deal.

For investors watching NYSE:UNP, this decision places one of the largest US freight rail operators in a holding pattern on a major deal. Union Pacific is a core player in long haul freight, and any change in its network or competitive position can matter for volumes, pricing, and capital plans. The regulator’s pushback highlights how closely rail consolidation is being reviewed at this time.

From here, the focus is on how Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern adjust their filing and timetable, and whether they modify deal terms or integration plans. The outcome is uncertain, and the process can influence how management prioritizes balance sheet flexibility, network investments, and potential alternatives if the merger case proves difficult to advance.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Union Pacific by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Union Pacific.

Is Union Pacific financially strong enough to weather the next crisis?

The Surface Transportation Board’s rejection of the first merger filing keeps Union Pacific’s largest potential deal in a holding pattern and introduces more uncertainty around timing, costs, and possible conditions. The regulators’ request for detailed market share projections and missing merger documents suggests the review may be data heavy and could require management attention that might otherwise go to network projects and shareholder returns.

Advertisement

Union Pacific’s Narrative Meets a Tougher Regulator

Union Pacific is often talked about as a steady, real economy infrastructure play with strong cash generation and reliable dividends, so a large merger was already a big swing compared with its usual slow burn story. The setback on the filing may reinforce the view that the core appeal still lies in the existing western US network and cash flows, rather than in transformational deals that depend on regulatory approvals.

Balancing Risks and Rewards After the STB Pushback

  • ⚠️ Regulatory risk has clearly risen, with a key ruling already putting the merger at “a little more risk” and raising questions over possible concessions or delays.
  • ⚠️ Management could face higher legal, advisory, and lobbying costs, alongside distraction from projects like the planned Mainline Texas Industrial Park.
  • 🎁 If the merger eventually proceeds, clearer data on market share and truck to rail shifts could support a more transparent case for long term freight volumes and efficiency.
  • 🎁 Even without the deal, Union Pacific’s role as a core freight rail operator with a long dividend track record continues to underpin its appeal for long horizon investors.

What to Watch Next

From here, the key things to track are how quickly Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern resubmit, what additional disclosures they provide on market share and competitive impact, and whether regulators hint at specific conditions or structural remedies. For a broader view on how other investors are thinking about this setback and the longer term story, you can read community perspectives in this investor discussion hub.

This article by Simply Wall St is general in nature. We provide commentary based on historical data and analyst forecasts only using an unbiased methodology and our articles are not intended to be financial advice. data.title: Assessing Fair Isaac (FICO) Valuation As GPU Optimization And AI Decisioning Progress Attract Fresh Attention
data.description:
Fair Isaac (FICO) recently drew fresh attention after updating its FICO Xpress Optimization suite with NVIDIA CUDA X GPU libraries. The move is aimed at tackling very large, complex computational problems more efficiently.

See our latest analysis for Fair Isaac.

Despite the product update, recent trading has been choppy, with a 1 day share price return of a 4.64% decline and a 30 day share price return of a 14.52% decline. The 1 year total shareholder return of a 21.10% decline contrasts with very strong 3 and 5 year total shareholder returns of 133.44% and 192.15%, suggesting long term momentum has been strong even as shorter term sentiment has cooled.

If this kind of AI focused software story interests you, it may be worth seeing what else is out there through high growth tech and AI stocks to spot other potential opportunities.

With Fair Isaac shares pulling back despite its entrenched position in credit scoring and a US$1,494.50 price sitting below the US$2,034.26 average analyst target, is there a genuine buying opportunity here or is future growth already priced in?

Advertisement

Price-to-Earnings of 54.5x: Is it justified?

On a simple headline number, Fair Isaac's P/E of 54.5x sits against a last close price of US$1,494.50 and suggests investors are paying a premium relative to many peers.

The P/E ratio tells you how much investors are currently willing to pay for each dollar of earnings, which matters a lot for a profitable software name like FICO. At 54.5x, the market is assigning a high price to those earnings, which often reflects expectations for continued growth and the value placed on its scoring and software franchises.

However, this premium is not small. FICO's 54.5x P/E is significantly above the US Software industry average of 30.9x and also above the peer average of 41.5x. This signals the market is pricing in stronger outcomes than many comparables. Against an estimated fair P/E of 38.3x, the current multiple also sits well above a level the market could move towards if sentiment or expectations cool.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Fair Isaac

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 54.5x (OVERVALUED)

However, you also have to weigh risks such as a sharp reversal in long term share price momentum and any slowdown in revenue or net income growth related to recent AI investments.

Find out about the key risks to this Fair Isaac narrative.

Another View: Our DCF Model Points to a Different Story

While the P/E of 54.5x suggests Fair Isaac looks expensive, our DCF model puts future cash flow value at US$1,424.58 versus the current US$1,494.50 share price. That implies the shares are slightly overvalued on cash flows too, so where is the margin of safety for new money?

data.title: Is Tuya’s (TUYA) Emotional AI Robotics Push Redefining Its Core Platform Strategy?
data.description:
• Earlier in January 2026 at CES, Tuya Smart unveiled Aura, an AI-powered pet companion robot that blends emotional monitoring, autonomous mobility, and GenAI-driven interaction, and also announced a partnership with Robopoet to launch Fuzozo, a cellular-connected AI emotional companion for always-on use beyond the home.
  • Together, Aura and the Robopoet collaboration signal Tuya’s push to extend its AI ecosystem from smart devices into emotionally aware, service-rich robotics that could touch pet care, home services, and broader everyday companionship use cases.
  • We’ll now examine how this move into AI-powered emotional companionship, particularly through Aura’s robotics platform, reshapes Tuya’s investment narrative.

These 9 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is Tuya's Investment Narrative?

For Tuya to make sense in a portfolio, you need to believe in its shift from a pure IoT platform to a broader AI ecosystem that can support recurring, service-driven revenue on top of hardware. The Aura robot and Robopoet partnership fit that story, extending Tuya’s AI into emotionally aware, always-connected companions, but they are unlikely to move the needle on the near-term numbers that underpin the current valuation, especially after a recent share pullback despite improving profitability and buybacks. In the short term, the key catalysts still look more prosaic: execution on AIoT partnerships, monetization of its developer base, and sustaining earnings quality while funding innovation. The biggest new risk is Tuya stretching into capital-intensive robotics and consumer hardware where competition is intense and demand uncertain.

However, there is one Aura-related risk that current shareholders should not overlook. Despite retreating, Tuya's shares might still be trading 11% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Thirteen Simply Wall St Community fair values for Tuya span roughly US$2.11 to US$8.09, reflecting very different expectations. Set that against Tuya’s push into AI companions and the risk of higher spend colliding with only modest earnings growth.

Explore 13 other fair value estimates on Tuya - why the stock might be worth just $2.11!

data.title: Is Pre-Earnings Trading Hinting At A Shift In B2Gold’s (TSX:BTO) Cost And Production Story?
data.description:
• B2Gold Corp. has attracted unusually heavy pre-market trading interest in recent days ahead of its earnings report, scheduled for 25 February 2026, which is expected to update investors on production levels, operating costs, and future guidance.
  • The heightened activity appears driven less by fresh corporate announcements and more by investors’ expectations around forthcoming operational and outlook details.
  • We’ll now examine how this earnings-related positioning, particularly around anticipated production and cost updates, could influence B2Gold’s broader investment narrative.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

What Is B2Gold's Investment Narrative?

To own B2Gold, you need to believe in its ability to convert growing production into sustainable cash flow while managing geopolitical exposure and ongoing capital demands. The upcoming February earnings, which have already sparked heavy pre-market interest, sit right at the heart of that story: investors are effectively “pre-positioning” around updated production, cost, and guidance details. If those numbers simply reaffirm existing guidance, the news may not fundamentally shift the main short term catalysts, which still center on execution at Fekola and Goose and disciplined spending. But a meaningful change in cost outlook, permitting timelines, or capex plans could sharpen attention on the key risks flagged previously, particularly country risk and the balance between dividends, buybacks, and growth projects.

B2Gold's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Thirteen fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly C$4.6 to about C$42.77, reflecting sharply different views on B2Gold’s long term earnings power. As you weigh those opinions, keep in mind how the upcoming earnings update on production, costs, and guidance could either reinforce or challenge the current focus on geopolitical and permitting risk, which may, in turn, influence how comfortably the market prices the stock’s growth projects.

data.title: Assessing Kraft Heinz (KHC) Valuation As Recent Weak Returns Contrast With Cash Flow Upside Potential
data.description:
Advertisement

What Kraft Heinz’s Recent Returns May Signal for Investors

Kraft Heinz (KHC) has seen its share price under pressure recently, with negative returns over the past month, past 3 months, year to date, and past year, even as annual revenue and net income figures remain positive.

See our latest analysis for Kraft Heinz.

At a share price of $23.76, Kraft Heinz’s momentum looks muted, with a 30 day share price return of a 3.06% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of a 14.58% loss, pointing to fading enthusiasm despite positive annual revenue and net income figures.

If this kind of mixed performance has you looking around the market, it could be a good moment to broaden your search with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Kraft Heinz trading at $23.76 and indicators like analyst targets and intrinsic value estimates suggesting a discount, the key question is whether this is a genuine value opportunity or whether the market already sees limited future growth.

Price-to-Sales of 1.1x: Is it justified?

On a P/S of 1.1x at a share price of $23.76, Kraft Heinz screens as good value against peers in some checks, but looks more expensive in others.

The P/S ratio compares what investors are paying for each dollar of revenue. It is often used for companies where earnings are weak or volatile. For Kraft Heinz, this tilt toward sales-based metrics fits with its current unprofitable status and the reliance on future earnings improvement rather than present profits.

Against its own fair P/S estimate of 1.3x, Kraft Heinz trades at a lower level. This suggests the market price is sitting below where our fair ratio work indicates it could settle. Yet when you stack that 1.1x P/S next to the broader US Food industry average of 0.8x, the stock looks more expensive than many peers, hinting that investors are still assigning a premium even with recent share price pressure.

However, you also need to weigh risks such as Kraft Heinz’s recent net income loss of $4.4b and a 30.7% three-year total shareholder return decline.

Another View: What the SWS DCF Model Suggests

While the P/S of 1.1x makes Kraft Heinz look roughly in line with its fair ratio work, our DCF model points in a different direction. It estimates future cash flows at $68.79 per share versus today’s $23.76 price, which flags a sizeable potential undervaluation.

That kind of gap can signal either an opportunity or that the cash flow assumptions are too optimistic, so it is worth asking which story you think fits Kraft Heinz better, and why.

data.title: Why We Like The Returns At Elmos Semiconductor (ETR:ELG)
data.description:
What are the early trends we should look for to identify a stock that could multiply in value over the long term? Ideally, a business will show two trends; firstly a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and secondly, an increasing amount of capital employed. Basically this means that a company has profitable initiatives that it can continue to reinvest in, which is a trait of a compounding machine. And in light of that, the trends we're seeing at Elmos Semiconductor's (ETR:ELG) look very promising so lets take a look.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Return On Capital Employed (ROCE): What Is It?

If you haven't worked with ROCE before, it measures the 'return' (pre-tax profit) a company generates from capital employed in its business. To calculate this metric for Elmos Semiconductor, this is the formula:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.22 = €157m ÷ (€811m - €84m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Therefore, Elmos Semiconductor has an ROCE of 22%. That's a fantastic return and not only that, it outpaces the average of 13% earned by companies in a similar industry.

View our latest analysis for Elmos Semiconductor

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Elmos Semiconductor compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering Elmos Semiconductor for free.

The Trend Of ROCE

We like the trends that we're seeing from Elmos Semiconductor. The numbers show that in the last five years, the returns generated on capital employed have grown considerably to 22%. The amount of capital employed has increased too, by 106%. This can indicate that there's plenty of opportunities to invest capital internally and at ever higher rates, a combination that's common among multi-baggers.

The Bottom Line On Elmos Semiconductor's ROCE

To sum it up, Elmos Semiconductor has proven it can reinvest in the business and generate higher returns on that capital employed, which is terrific. And a remarkable 249% total return over the last five years tells us that investors are expecting more good things to come in the future. In light of that, we think it's worth looking further into this stock because if Elmos Semiconductor can keep these trends up, it could have a bright future ahead.

Like most companies, Elmos Semiconductor does come with some risks, and we've found 1 warning sign that you should be aware of.

If you'd like to see other companies earning high returns, check out our free list of companies earning high returns with solid balance sheets here.

data.title: Botnia Gold AB (publ) (STO:BOTX) Stocks Shoot Up 25% But Its P/S Still Looks Reasonable
data.description:
Those holding Botnia Gold AB (publ) (STO:BOTX) shares would be relieved that the share price has rebounded 25% in the last thirty days, but it needs to keep going to repair the recent damage it has caused to investor portfolios. Looking further back, the 13% rise over the last twelve months isn't too bad notwithstanding the strength over the last 30 days.

After such a large jump in price, given close to half the companies operating in Sweden's Metals and Mining industry have price-to-sales ratios (or "P/S") below 2.1x, you may consider Botnia Gold as a stock to potentially avoid with its 3x P/S ratio. However, the P/S might be high for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Check out our latest analysis for Botnia Gold

Advertisement

What Does Botnia Gold's P/S Mean For Shareholders?

With revenue growth that's exceedingly strong of late, Botnia Gold has been doing very well. The P/S ratio is probably high because investors think this strong revenue growth will be enough to outperform the broader industry in the near future. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

Want the full picture on earnings, revenue and cash flow for the company? Then our free report on Botnia Gold will help you shine a light on its historical performance.

Do Revenue Forecasts Match The High P/S Ratio?

Botnia Gold's P/S ratio would be typical for a company that's expected to deliver solid growth, and importantly, perform better than the industry.

Taking a look back first, we see that the company's revenues underwent some rampant growth over the last 12 months. The latest three year period has also seen an incredible overall rise in revenue, aided by its incredible short-term performance. Accordingly, shareholders would have been over the moon with those medium-term rates of revenue growth.

Comparing that recent medium-term revenue trajectory with the industry's one-year growth forecast of 11% shows it's noticeably more attractive.

With this information, we can see why Botnia Gold is trading at such a high P/S compared to the industry. Presumably shareholders aren't keen to offload something they believe will continue to outmanoeuvre the wider industry.

What Does Botnia Gold's P/S Mean For Investors?

Botnia Gold's P/S is on the rise since its shares have risen strongly. While the price-to-sales ratio shouldn't be the defining factor in whether you buy a stock or not, it's quite a capable barometer of revenue expectations.

We've established that Botnia Gold maintains its high P/S on the strength of its recent three-year growth being higher than the wider industry forecast, as expected. At this stage investors feel the potential continued revenue growth in the future is great enough to warrant an inflated P/S. Barring any significant changes to the company's ability to make money, the share price should continue to be propped up.

You should always think about risks. Case in point, we've spotted 2 warning signs for Botnia Gold you should be aware of, and 1 of them is potentially serious.

If companies with solid past earnings growth is up your alley, you may wish to see this free collection of other companies with strong earnings growth and low P/E ratios.

data.title: AfroCentric Investment (JSE:ACT) shareholders are up 12% this past week, but still in the red over the last three years
data.description:
AfroCentric Investment Corporation Limited (JSE:ACT) shareholders should be happy to see the share price up 19% in the last month. But that doesn't change the fact that the returns over the last three years have been stomach churning. The share price has sunk like a leaky ship, down 72% in that time. Arguably, the recent bounce is to be expected after such a bad drop. Only time will tell if the company can sustain the turnaround.

While the stock has risen 12% in the past week but long term shareholders are still in the red, let's see what the fundamentals can tell us.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

There is no denying that markets are sometimes efficient, but prices do not always reflect underlying business performance. One imperfect but simple way to consider how the market perception of a company has shifted is to compare the change in the earnings per share (EPS) with the share price movement.

Over the three years that the share price declined, AfroCentric Investment's earnings per share (EPS) dropped significantly, falling to a loss. This was, in part, due to extraordinary items impacting earnings. Due to the loss, it's not easy to use EPS as a reliable guide to the business. However, we can say we'd expect to see a falling share price in this scenario.

You can see how EPS has changed over time in the image below (click on the chart to see the exact values).

Before buying or selling a stock, we always recommend a close examination of historic growth trends, available here.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

As well as measuring the share price return, investors should also consider the total shareholder return (TSR). The TSR is a return calculation that accounts for the value of cash dividends (assuming that any dividend received was reinvested) and the calculated value of any discounted capital raisings and spin-offs. It's fair to say that the TSR gives a more complete picture for stocks that pay a dividend. In the case of AfroCentric Investment, it has a TSR of -70% for the last 3 years. That exceeds its share price return that we previously mentioned. The dividends paid by the company have thusly boosted the total shareholder return.

A Different Perspective

Investors in AfroCentric Investment had a tough year, with a total loss of 22% (including dividends), against a market gain of about 43%. Even the share prices of good stocks drop sometimes, but we want to see improvements in the fundamental metrics of a business, before getting too interested. Regrettably, last year's performance caps off a bad run, with the shareholders facing a total loss of 10% per year over five years. Generally speaking long term share price weakness can be a bad sign, though contrarian investors might want to research the stock in hope of a turnaround. It's always interesting to track share price performance over the longer term. But to understand AfroCentric Investment better, we need to consider many other factors. Take risks, for example - AfroCentric Investment has 4 warning signs (and 3 which are concerning) we think you should know about.

If you are like me, then you will not want to miss this free list of undervalued small caps that insiders are buying.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on South African exchanges.

data.title: Hoist Finance AB (publ)'s (STO:HOFI) biggest owners are private companies who got richer after stock soared 10% last week
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Hoist Finance's significant private companies ownership suggests that the key decisions are influenced by shareholders from the larger public
  • The top 5 shareholders own 55% of the company
  • 20% of Hoist Finance is held by insiders

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

If you want to know who really controls Hoist Finance AB (publ) (STO:HOFI), then you'll have to look at the makeup of its share registry. With 28% stake, private companies possess the maximum shares in the company. That is, the group stands to benefit the most if the stock rises (or lose the most if there is a downturn).

As a result, private companies collectively scored the highest last week as the company hit kr11b market cap following a 10% gain in the stock.

In the chart below, we zoom in on the different ownership groups of Hoist Finance.

View our latest analysis for Hoist Finance

What Does The Institutional Ownership Tell Us About Hoist Finance?

Institutional investors commonly compare their own returns to the returns of a commonly followed index. So they generally do consider buying larger companies that are included in the relevant benchmark index.

As you can see, institutional investors have a fair amount of stake in Hoist Finance. This implies the analysts working for those institutions have looked at the stock and they like it. But just like anyone else, they could be wrong. When multiple institutions own a stock, there's always a risk that they are in a 'crowded trade'. When such a trade goes wrong, multiple parties may compete to sell stock fast. This risk is higher in a company without a history of growth. You can see Hoist Finance's historic earnings and revenue below, but keep in mind there's always more to the story.

Hoist Finance is not owned by hedge funds. Erik Selin Fastigheter AB is currently the largest shareholder, with 22% of shares outstanding. In comparison, the second and third largest shareholders hold about 17% and 5.9% of the stock. Furthermore, CEO Harry Vranjes is the owner of 0.8% of the company's shares.

On looking further, we found that 55% of the shares are owned by the top 5 shareholders. In other words, these shareholders have a meaningful say in the decisions of the company.

While it makes sense to study institutional ownership data for a company, it also makes sense to study analyst sentiments to know which way the wind is blowing. There are plenty of analysts covering the stock, so it might be worth seeing what they are forecasting, too.

Insider Ownership Of Hoist Finance

While the precise definition of an insider can be subjective, almost everyone considers board members to be insiders. The company management answer to the board and the latter should represent the interests of shareholders. Notably, sometimes top-level managers are on the board themselves.

I generally consider insider ownership to be a good thing. However, on some occasions it makes it more difficult for other shareholders to hold the board accountable for decisions.

Our information suggests that insiders maintain a significant holding in Hoist Finance AB (publ). Insiders own kr2.2b worth of shares in the kr11b company. That's quite meaningful. Most would be pleased to see the board is investing alongside them. You may wish to access this free chart showing recent trading by insiders.

General Public Ownership

The general public, who are usually individual investors, hold a 27% stake in Hoist Finance. While this group can't necessarily call the shots, it can certainly have a real influence on how the company is run.

Private Company Ownership

It seems that Private Companies own 28%, of the Hoist Finance stock. It's hard to draw any conclusions from this fact alone, so its worth looking into who owns those private companies. Sometimes insiders or other related parties have an interest in shares in a public company through a separate private company.

Next Steps:

I find it very interesting to look at who exactly owns a company. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. For instance, we've identified 1 warning sign for Hoist Finance that you should be aware of.

If you would prefer discover what analysts are predicting in terms of future growth, do not miss this free report on analyst forecasts.

NB: Figures in this article are calculated using data from the last twelve months, which refer to the 12-month period ending on the last date of the month the financial statement is dated. This may not be consistent with full year annual report figures.

data.title: If EPS Growth Is Important To You, Hera (BIT:HER) Presents An Opportunity
data.description:
For beginners, it can seem like a good idea (and an exciting prospect) to buy a company that tells a good story to investors, even if it currently lacks a track record of revenue and profit. But the reality is that when a company loses money each year, for long enough, its investors will usually take their share of those losses. Loss-making companies are always racing against time to reach financial sustainability, so investors in these companies may be taking on more risk than they should.

Despite being in the age of tech-stock blue-sky investing, many investors still adopt a more traditional strategy; buying shares in profitable companies like Hera (BIT:HER). Even if this company is fairly valued by the market, investors would agree that generating consistent profits will continue to provide Hera with the means to add long-term value to shareholders.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

How Quickly Is Hera Increasing Earnings Per Share?

Generally, companies experiencing growth in earnings per share (EPS) should see similar trends in share price. Therefore, there are plenty of investors who like to buy shares in companies that are growing EPS. Hera's shareholders have have plenty to be happy about as their annual EPS growth for the last 3 years was 57%. While that sort of growth rate isn't sustainable for long, it certainly catches the eye of prospective investors.

Careful consideration of revenue growth and earnings before interest and taxation (EBIT) margins can help inform a view on the sustainability of the recent profit growth. Hera maintained stable EBIT margins over the last year, all while growing revenue 10.0% to €14b. That's progress.

The chart below shows how the company's bottom and top lines have progressed over time. For finer detail, click on the image.

See our latest analysis for Hera

Fortunately, we've got access to analyst forecasts of Hera's future profits. You can do your own forecasts without looking, or you can take a peek at what the professionals are predicting.

Are Hera Insiders Aligned With All Shareholders?

It's a good habit to check into a company's remuneration policies to ensure that the CEO and management team aren't putting their own interests before that of the shareholder with excessive salary packages. The median total compensation for CEOs of companies similar in size to Hera, with market caps between €3.4b and €10b, is around €1.7m.

The Hera CEO received total compensation of just €810k in the year to December 2024. That looks like a modest pay packet, and may hint at a certain respect for the interests of shareholders. While the level of CEO compensation shouldn't be the biggest factor in how the company is viewed, modest remuneration is a positive, because it suggests that the board keeps shareholder interests in mind. Generally, arguments can be made that reasonable pay levels attest to good decision-making.

Is Hera Worth Keeping An Eye On?

Hera's earnings have taken off in quite an impressive fashion. Such fast EPS growth prompts the question: has the business reached an inflection point? At the same time the reasonable CEO compensation reflects well on the board of directors. So Hera looks like it could be a good quality growth stock, at first glance. That's worth watching. However, before you get too excited we've discovered 2 warning signs for Hera that you should be aware of.

While opting for stocks without growing earnings and absent insider buying can yield results, for investors valuing these key metrics, here is a carefully selected list of companies in IT with promising growth potential and insider confidence.

Please note the insider transactions discussed in this article refer to reportable transactions in the relevant jurisdiction.

data.title: Assessing Wendel (ENXTPA:MF) Valuation After Recent Muted Share Performance And Modest DCF Upside
data.description:
Advertisement

Context for Wendel’s Recent Share Performance

With no single headline event driving attention, Wendel (ENXTPA:MF) is drawing interest as investors weigh its recent share performance, its current €80.45 price and the firm’s role as a diversified private equity investor.

See our latest analysis for Wendel.

Recent moves have been relatively muted, with a 1 day share price return of 1.58% and a year to date share price return decline of 1.47%, while the 1 year total shareholder return decline of 12.28% points to fading longer term momentum.

If Wendel’s recent moves have you reassessing opportunities, it could be a good moment to widen your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With the shares at €80.45, trading at a discount to the €110.50 analyst price target and with only a modest intrinsic discount of around 4%, you have to ask: is Wendel undervalued, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Price-to-Sales of 0.4x: Is it justified?

On a simple P/S of 0.4x at a share price of €80.45, Wendel screens as inexpensive relative to both its direct peers and the wider European diversified financials group.

The P/S multiple compares the value the market is placing on the company to its revenue, which can be helpful when earnings are currently negative, as they are for Wendel. For an investment company with a mix of listed and unlisted holdings, using sales as the reference point can give you a cleaner, less volatile yardstick than profits alone.

Wendel trades on a P/S of 0.4x compared with about 2.4x for the European diversified financial industry and around 4.5x for its peer group, a steep gap that suggests the market is assigning a far lower value to each euro of Wendel’s revenue. Compared with an estimated fair P/S of 1.6x, the current level is also well below where the ratio could gravitate if sentiment and fundamentals were more closely aligned.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Wendel

Result: Price-to-Sales of 0.4x (UNDERVALUED)

However, you still have to weigh risks such as the current €90 million net loss and Wendel’s exposure to larger, concentrated equity investments across cycles.

Find out about the key risks to this Wendel narrative.

Another View: What Our DCF Model Says

While the 0.4x P/S ratio flags Wendel as inexpensive, our DCF model paints a softer picture, with an estimated fair value of €83.71 versus the current €80.45 price, a discount of about 3.9%. That suggests less of a clear bargain and more of a fine margin. How much comfort do you take from such a slim gap?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Wendel for example). We show the entire calculation in full. data.title: What Anheuser-Busch InBev (ENXTBR:ABI)'s Exclusive Live Nation UK Partnership Means For Shareholders
data.description:
• In January 2026, Live Nation announced AB InBev as the exclusive beer and cider partner across major UK festivals, venues, and outdoor shows, putting brands like Budweiser, Corona, San Miguel and Stella Artois at the centre of the live music scene for fans of legal drinking age.
  • The partnership deepens a long-running global relationship between the brewer and the live entertainment group, giving AB InBev extensive on-the-ground and digital exposure at more than 20 festivals and Academy Music Group venues such as O2 Academy Brixton and O2 Shepherd's Bush Empire.
  • We’ll now examine how this exclusive UK Live Nation partnership, especially Budweiser’s elevated role in live music, shapes AB InBev’s investment narrative.

What Is Anheuser-Busch InBev's Investment Narrative?

For AB InBev, the big-picture belief is that a global portfolio of mainstream and premium beer brands can keep attracting volume and pricing power, while a large debt load is steadily reduced and cash is returned through dividends and buybacks. Recent results show a sizeable and profitable business, but with growth that is not especially fast and a return on equity that remains in single digits. Against that backdrop, the new exclusive Live Nation partnership in the UK looks more like a brand and distribution enhancer than a likely game changer for short term financial catalysts, although it does reinforce Budweiser’s role in live entertainment and could support incremental volume and mix in that market. The more immediate swing factors still look to be debt management, margin resilience and execution in key regions.

However, investors should not ignore how high debt and modest returns limit room for error. Anheuser-Busch InBev's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Community members on Simply Wall St place AB InBev’s fair value between US$51.93 and US$142.92, across 11 viewpoints, underlining how far opinions can stretch. data.title: Ocugen (OCGN) Is Down 12.6% After Positive Phase 2 OCU410 Data in Geographic Atrophy
data.description:
• Earlier in January 2026, Ocugen announced positive preliminary 12‑month Phase 2 ArMaDa data for OCU410 in geographic atrophy secondary to dry age‑related macular degeneration, showing reduced lesion growth and favorable safety across evaluated patients.
  • An interesting highlight is that medium doses produced greater lesion growth reduction than high doses, and no serious OCU410‑related adverse events were reported in either Phase 1 or Phase 2 so far.
  • Next, we’ll assess how this early OCU410 efficacy signal in a large, underserved geographic atrophy population could influence Ocugen’s investment narrative.

What Is Ocugen's Investment Narrative?

To own Ocugen today, you have to believe its modifier gene therapy platform can turn promising early science into a viable business before the cash clock runs down. The new 12‑month Phase 2 OCU410 data in geographic atrophy strengthens the bull case on the science side, giving Ocugen a more credible lead asset in a large, underserved disease where current treatments are burdensome and limited. In the near term, that makes upcoming regulatory feedback, any partnership interest and clearer Phase 3 plans more central catalysts than before. At the same time, Ocugen remains a small, loss‑making biotech with less than a year of cash runway and a history of raising equity, so financing risk and potential dilution still sit right at the heart of the story.

data.title: Merck Weighs Revolution Medicines Buyout And Expands AI Oncology Bet
data.description:
• Merck (NYSE:MRK) is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire Revolution Medicines in one of the largest biotech deals in recent years.
  • The potential acquisition would expand Merck's oncology pipeline, with a focus on targeted cancer therapies.
  • Merck is also a founding partner in Illumina's new AI driven pharmacogenomics initiative.
  • Recent collaborations with ChemLex and Guardant Health point to a broader push into AI enabled drug discovery and precision oncology.

For investors, this cluster of moves suggests that Merck is leaning further into oncology and data heavy R&D. The company already has a significant presence in cancer treatments, and adding Revolution Medicines together with AI and genomics partnerships could influence how its future pipeline is built.

These developments may affect how investors think about Merck's long term growth drivers, its capital allocation priorities, and its positioning relative to other large drugmakers. An important question will be how effectively Merck converts these deals and collaborations into clinically meaningful drugs and viable new revenue streams.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Merck by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Merck.

Why Merck could be great value

For investors, this cluster of deals points to Merck leaning harder into targeted oncology and data heavy R&D, with the possible US$28b to US$32b Revolution Medicines buyout sitting alongside AI, genomics and automation alliances as part of the same theme. If these talks progress, the price paid, the structure of any earn outs, and how Revolution’s RAS focused pipeline is slotted next to Merck’s existing oncology assets will likely shape how the market views management’s capital discipline and long term growth ambitions.

Advertisement

Merck Narrative, Reinforced Or Stretched?

Recent commentary around Merck has focused on building an oncology portfolio that can support growth as key products mature, and this move fits squarely into that story of heavy investment in cancer, data and platforms. For anyone following the Merck narrative as a free cash flow generator that can fund sizable deals while maintaining dividends, this potential acquisition and the Illumina, Guardant and ChemLex tie ups give more concrete evidence of where management is choosing to lean in.

Risks and rewards investors should weigh

  • Multiple alliances with Illumina, Guardant and ChemLex point to a broad push into AI, genomics and automation that could widen Merck’s future oncology and precision medicine toolkit.
  • A Revolution Medicines deal, if completed, would add late stage targeted cancer programs that are aligned with Merck’s existing oncology focus.
  • A US$28b to US$32b acquisition would be a large use of capital, so investors will likely scrutinize integration risk and whether returns ultimately justify the outlay.
  • Heavy reliance on external partnerships and a big biotech deal increases execution risk across clinical, regulatory and commercial stages.

What to watch next

From here, the key signals are whether Merck confirms exclusive terms with Revolution Medicines, how it outlines financial and pipeline impacts if a deal is agreed, and what it shares about timelines and early data from the Illumina, Guardant and ChemLex collaborations on upcoming calls and presentations. Investors who want to track how these moves feed into the wider story around Merck’s future growth drivers and risk profile can follow ongoing debate and analysis in the community narrative hub.

data.title: There's No Escaping Examobile S.A.'s (WSE:EXA) Muted Earnings Despite A 29% Share Price Rise
data.description:
Examobile S.A. (WSE:EXA) shares have had a really impressive month, gaining 29% after a shaky period beforehand. The last 30 days bring the annual gain to a very sharp 29%.

In spite of the firm bounce in price, Examobile may still be sending bullish signals at the moment with its price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 9.2x, since almost half of all companies in Poland have P/E ratios greater than 14x and even P/E's higher than 25x are not unusual. Although, it's not wise to just take the P/E at face value as there may be an explanation why it's limited.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Examobile has been doing a good job lately as it's been growing earnings at a solid pace. It might be that many expect the respectable earnings performance to degrade substantially, which has repressed the P/E. If you like the company, you'd be hoping this isn't the case so that you could potentially pick up some stock while it's out of favour.

See our latest analysis for Examobile

Although there are no analyst estimates available for Examobile, take a look at this free data-rich visualisation to see how the company stacks up on earnings, revenue and cash flow.
Advertisement

Is There Any Growth For Examobile?

There's an inherent assumption that a company should underperform the market for P/E ratios like Examobile's to be considered reasonable.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered a decent 15% gain to the company's bottom line. Still, lamentably EPS has fallen 28% in aggregate from three years ago, which is disappointing. Accordingly, shareholders would have felt downbeat about the medium-term rates of earnings growth.

Comparing that to the market, which is predicted to deliver 18% growth in the next 12 months, the company's downward momentum based on recent medium-term earnings results is a sobering picture.

In light of this, it's understandable that Examobile's P/E would sit below the majority of other companies. Nonetheless, there's no guarantee the P/E has reached a floor yet with earnings going in reverse. Even just maintaining these prices could be difficult to achieve as recent earnings trends are already weighing down the shares.

What We Can Learn From Examobile's P/E?

Examobile's stock might have been given a solid boost, but its P/E certainly hasn't reached any great heights. We'd say the price-to-earnings ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

As we suspected, our examination of Examobile revealed its shrinking earnings over the medium-term are contributing to its low P/E, given the market is set to grow. Right now shareholders are accepting the low P/E as they concede future earnings probably won't provide any pleasant surprises. Unless the recent medium-term conditions improve, they will continue to form a barrier for the share price around these levels.

Plus, you should also learn about these 4 warning signs we've spotted with Examobile (including 3 which make us uncomfortable).

If these risks are making you reconsider your opinion on Examobile, explore our interactive list of high quality stocks to get an idea of what else is out there.

data.title: Wüstenrot & Württembergische (ETR:WUW) shareholders are still up 26% over 1 year despite pulling back 3.1% in the past week
data.description:
These days it's easy to simply buy an index fund, and your returns should (roughly) match the market. But if you pick the right individual stocks, you could make more than that. To wit, the Wüstenrot & Württembergische AG (ETR:WUW) share price is 21% higher than it was a year ago, much better than the market return of around 10.0% (not including dividends) in the same period. If it can keep that out-performance up over the long term, investors will do very well! Zooming out, the stock is actually down 14% in the last three years.

While the stock has fallen 3.1% this week, it's worth focusing on the longer term and seeing if the stocks historical returns have been driven by the underlying fundamentals.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

While markets are a powerful pricing mechanism, share prices reflect investor sentiment, not just underlying business performance. By comparing earnings per share (EPS) and share price changes over time, we can get a feel for how investor attitudes to a company have morphed over time.

During the last year Wüstenrot & Württembergische grew its earnings per share, moving from a loss to a profit.

When a company has just transitioned to profitability, earnings per share growth is not always the best way to look at the share price action.

Absent any improvement, we don't think a thirst for dividends is pushing up the Wüstenrot & Württembergische's share price. It seems far more likely that the 6.9% boost to the revenue over the last year, is making the difference. Revenue growth often does precede earnings growth, so some investors might be willing to forgo profits today because they have their eyes fixed firmly on the future.

You can see how earnings and revenue have changed over time in the image below (click on the chart to see the exact values).

We know that Wüstenrot & Württembergische has improved its bottom line lately, but what does the future have in store? This free report showing analyst forecasts should help you form a view on Wüstenrot & Württembergische

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

It is important to consider the total shareholder return, as well as the share price return, for any given stock. The TSR is a return calculation that accounts for the value of cash dividends (assuming that any dividend received was reinvested) and the calculated value of any discounted capital raisings and spin-offs. It's fair to say that the TSR gives a more complete picture for stocks that pay a dividend. We note that for Wüstenrot & Württembergische the TSR over the last 1 year was 26%, which is better than the share price return mentioned above. This is largely a result of its dividend payments!

A Different Perspective

We're pleased to report that Wüstenrot & Württembergische shareholders have received a total shareholder return of 26% over one year. Of course, that includes the dividend. That's better than the annualised return of 1.1% over half a decade, implying that the company is doing better recently. Someone with an optimistic perspective could view the recent improvement in TSR as indicating that the business itself is getting better with time. Importantly, we haven't analysed Wüstenrot & Württembergische's dividend history. This free visual report on its dividends is a must-read if you're thinking of buying.

We will like Wüstenrot & Württembergische better if we see some big insider buys. While we wait, check out this free list of undervalued stocks (mostly small caps) with considerable, recent, insider buying.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on German exchanges.

data.title: 99% Of This Deneb Investments Insider's Holdings Were Sold
data.description:
Insiders were net sellers of Deneb Investments Limited's (JSE:DNB ) stock during the past year. That is, insiders sold more stock than they bought.

Although we don't think shareholders should simply follow insider transactions, we would consider it foolish to ignore insider transactions altogether.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Deneb Investments Insider Transactions Over The Last Year

In the last twelve months, the biggest single sale by an insider was when the Non-Executive Director, David Duncan, sold R3.0m worth of shares at a price of R2.50 per share. That means that an insider was selling shares at slightly below the current price (R2.64). When an insider sells below the current price, it suggests that they considered that lower price to be fair. That makes us wonder what they think of the (higher) recent valuation. While insider selling is not a positive sign, we can't be sure if it does mean insiders think the shares are fully valued, so it's only a weak sign. We note that the biggest single sale was 99% of David Duncan's holding. David Duncan was the only individual insider to sell shares in the last twelve months.

You can see the insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year depicted in the chart below. By clicking on the graph below, you can see the precise details of each insider transaction!

View our latest analysis for Deneb Investments

For those who like to find hidden gems this free list of small cap companies with recent insider purchasing, could be just the ticket.

Does Deneb Investments Boast High Insider Ownership?

I like to look at how many shares insiders own in a company, to help inform my view of how aligned they are with insiders. We usually like to see fairly high levels of insider ownership. Based on our data, Deneb Investments insiders have about 2.3% of the stock, worth approximately R27m. We consider this fairly low insider ownership.

So What Does This Data Suggest About Deneb Investments Insiders?

The fact that there have been no Deneb Investments insider transactions recently certainly doesn't bother us. Still, the insider transactions at Deneb Investments in the last 12 months are not very heartening. The modest level of insider ownership is, at least, some comfort. While it's good to be aware of what's going on with the insider's ownership and transactions, we make sure to also consider what risks are facing a stock before making any investment decision. For example - Deneb Investments has 2 warning signs we think you should be aware of.

Of course, you might find a fantastic investment by looking elsewhere. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies.

For the purposes of this article, insiders are those individuals who report their transactions to the relevant regulatory body. We currently account for open market transactions and private dispositions of direct interests only, but not derivative transactions or indirect interests.

data.title: Improved Earnings Required Before Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING S.A. (WSE:FSG) Shares Find Their Feet
data.description:
When close to half the companies in Poland have price-to-earnings ratios (or "P/E's") above 14x, you may consider Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING S.A. (WSE:FSG) as a highly attractive investment with its 4.5x P/E ratio. However, the P/E might be quite low for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

For example, consider that Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING's financial performance has been poor lately as its earnings have been in decline. One possibility is that the P/E is low because investors think the company won't do enough to avoid underperforming the broader market in the near future. If you like the company, you'd be hoping this isn't the case so that you could potentially pick up some stock while it's out of favour.

View our latest analysis for Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING

Want the full picture on earnings, revenue and cash flow for the company? Then our free report on Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING will help you shine a light on its historical performance.
Advertisement

How Is Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING's Growth Trending?

There's an inherent assumption that a company should far underperform the market for P/E ratios like Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING's to be considered reasonable.

If we review the last year of earnings, dishearteningly the company's profits fell to the tune of 10%. That put a dampener on the good run it was having over the longer-term as its three-year EPS growth is still a noteworthy 25% in total. So we can start by confirming that the company has generally done a good job of growing earnings over that time, even though it had some hiccups along the way.

This is in contrast to the rest of the market, which is expected to grow by 18% over the next year, materially higher than the company's recent medium-term annualised growth rates.

In light of this, it's understandable that Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING's P/E sits below the majority of other companies. It seems most investors are expecting to see the recent limited growth rates continue into the future and are only willing to pay a reduced amount for the stock.

The Final Word

We'd say the price-to-earnings ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

As we suspected, our examination of Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING revealed its three-year earnings trends are contributing to its low P/E, given they look worse than current market expectations. Right now shareholders are accepting the low P/E as they concede future earnings probably won't provide any pleasant surprises. Unless the recent medium-term conditions improve, they will continue to form a barrier for the share price around these levels.

There are also other vital risk factors to consider before investing and we've discovered 1 warning sign for Fabryki Sprzetu i Narzedzi Górniczych Grupa Kapitalowa FASING that you should be aware of.

data.title: Lahav LR Real Estate Ltd's (TLV:LAHAV) market cap increased by ₪183m, insiders receive a 55% cut
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Lahav LR Real Estate's significant insider ownership suggests inherent interests in company's expansion
  • The top 3 shareholders own 54% of the company
  • 14% of Lahav LR Real Estate is held by Institutions

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

If you want to know who really controls Lahav LR Real Estate Ltd (TLV:LAHAV), then you'll have to look at the makeup of its share registry. And the group that holds the biggest piece of the pie are individual insiders with 55% ownership. That is, the group stands to benefit the most if the stock rises (or lose the most if there is a downturn).

As a result, insiders scored the highest last week as the company hit ₪2.7b market cap following a 7.3% gain in the stock.

Let's take a closer look to see what the different types of shareholders can tell us about Lahav LR Real Estate.

See our latest analysis for Lahav LR Real Estate

What Does The Institutional Ownership Tell Us About Lahav LR Real Estate?

Institutional investors commonly compare their own returns to the returns of a commonly followed index. So they generally do consider buying larger companies that are included in the relevant benchmark index.

Lahav LR Real Estate already has institutions on the share registry. Indeed, they own a respectable stake in the company. This implies the analysts working for those institutions have looked at the stock and they like it. But just like anyone else, they could be wrong. If multiple institutions change their view on a stock at the same time, you could see the share price drop fast. It's therefore worth looking at Lahav LR Real Estate's earnings history below. Of course, the future is what really matters.

Lahav LR Real Estate is not owned by hedge funds. Looking at our data, we can see that the largest shareholder is Avraham Levy with 38% of shares outstanding. With 8.7% and 7.0% of the shares outstanding respectively, Intergemel Provident Funds and Pension Ltd. and Naftali Shimshon are the second and third largest shareholders. Naftali Shimshon, who is the third-largest shareholder, also happens to hold the title of Member of the Board of Directors. In addition, we found that Hillel Rozanski, the CEO has 2.0% of the shares allocated to their name.

To make our study more interesting, we found that the top 3 shareholders have a majority ownership in the company, meaning that they are powerful enough to influence the decisions of the company.

While it makes sense to study institutional ownership data for a company, it also makes sense to study analyst sentiments to know which way the wind is blowing. We're not picking up on any analyst coverage of the stock at the moment, so the company is unlikely to be widely held.

Insider Ownership Of Lahav LR Real Estate

The definition of an insider can differ slightly between different countries, but members of the board of directors always count. Company management run the business, but the CEO will answer to the board, even if he or she is a member of it.

Insider ownership is positive when it signals leadership are thinking like the true owners of the company. However, high insider ownership can also give immense power to a small group within the company. This can be negative in some circumstances.

It seems that insiders own more than half the Lahav LR Real Estate Ltd stock. This gives them a lot of power. That means they own ₪1.5b worth of shares in the ₪2.7b company. That's quite meaningful. Most would be pleased to see the board is investing alongside them. You may wish todiscover (for free) if they have been buying or selling.

General Public Ownership

With a 24% ownership, the general public, mostly comprising of individual investors, have some degree of sway over Lahav LR Real Estate. This size of ownership, while considerable, may not be enough to change company policy if the decision is not in sync with other large shareholders.

Private Company Ownership

We can see that Private Companies own 6.8%, of the shares on issue. It's hard to draw any conclusions from this fact alone, so its worth looking into who owns those private companies. Sometimes insiders or other related parties have an interest in shares in a public company through a separate private company.

Next Steps:

While it is well worth considering the different groups that own a company, there are other factors that are even more important. For example, we've discovered 3 warning signs for Lahav LR Real Estate (1 can't be ignored!) that you should be aware of before investing here.

If you would prefer check out another company -- one with potentially superior financials -- then do not miss this free list of interesting companies, backed by strong financial data.

NB: Figures in this article are calculated using data from the last twelve months, which refer to the 12-month period ending on the last date of the month the financial statement is dated. This may not be consistent with full year annual report figures.
